fields.summary: API changes required to support recurring reservations in the YARN ReservationSystem
Support for recurring reservations in the YARN ReservationSystem

fields.description: YARN-5326 proposes adding native support for recurring reservations in the YARN ReservationSystem. This JIRA is a sub-task to track the changes needed in ApplicationClientProtocol to accomplish it. Please refer to the design doc in the parent JIRA for details.

resolution.description: A fix for this issue is checked into the tree and tested.
resolution.name: Fixed

comments.body: This is an API change to add a period associated with periodic reservation requests. Period is represented in milliseconds for periodic jobs and has a value of 0 for non-periodic jobs. There are no test files since this is only an API change.

Thanks [~ajsangeetha] for the patch. It mostly looks good, a few minor comments:
  * We need to add more meat to the Javadocs for {{ReservationDefinition::get/setPeriodicity}} like how we distinguish periodic/non-periodic, implicit priority in the event of replanning, consistency of allocation, good till cancelled aspect etc.
  * Can you add an explicit default value for *period* in {{yarn_protos.proto}}.
  * Maybe it makes sense to add *period* to {{ReservationDefinitionPBImpl::toString}}.
Thanks for the comments, Subru. I have addressed all of them and added a new patch.

Please note that the failed test is unrelated to this patch.

Thanks [~ajsangeetha], this patch looks good to me, except for a few comments:

1. We need to add periodicity to REST API as well.
2. REST API changes will need a corresponding change to the documentation.* You can take a look at YARN-4683 for details. 

*It might make sense to add documentation of this API when the feature is code complete, similar to what was done for YARN-4340.
Thanks [~seanpo03]. I will add periodicity to REST API and make necessary changes in documentation when the feature is completed.

Is it a valid use case to say reserve some resource for different recurring, such as everyday, every workday, Tue/Thu every week, etc. Just like sending a calendar from outlook? It could be helpful to support richer semantics for recurring reservation, otherwise I'm afraid it could be hard to add them in the future.

I had a more detailed offline discussion with [~leftnoteasy] and we decided that the most future proof approach would be to have *period* as a _String_ rather than _long_.

In the first version, we will only accept long values (we will directly parse to long and reject if it fails) and in future we can support more richer calendaring semantics via regexes. We should explicitly call this out in the Javadocs.

Please note that the failed test is unrelated to this patch.

Thanks [~ajsangeetha], this patch looks good to me, except for a few comments:

1. We need to add periodicity to REST API as well.
2. REST API changes will need a corresponding change to the documentation.* You can take a look at YARN-4683 for details. 

*It might make sense to add documentation of this API when the feature is code complete, similar to what was done for YARN-4340.
Thanks [~seanpo03]. I will add periodicity to REST API and make necessary changes in documentation when the feature is completed.

Is it a valid use case to say reserve some resource for different recurring, such as everyday, every workday, Tue/Thu every week, etc. Just like sending a calendar from outlook? It could be helpful to support richer semantics for recurring reservation, otherwise I'm afraid it could be hard to add them in the future.

I had a more detailed offline discussion with [~leftnoteasy] and we decided that the most future proof approach would be to have *period* as a _String_ rather than _long_.

In the first version, we will only accept long values (we will directly parse to long and reject if it fails) and in future we can support more richer calendaring semantics via regexes. We should explicitly call this out in the Javadocs.
